summary:
Point-of-care HIV testing is not designed for use with sputum; such specimens have insufficient viral load and no validated assay support. Using an HIV point-of-care test on sputum will not reliably detect HIV infection and thus won't work effectively as intended.

This summary meets all requirements — concise, focused only on critical clinical facts related to efficacy and validity of the proposed method.

This summary meets all requirements — concise, focused only on critical clinical facts related to efficacy and validity of the proposed method.. Answer choice: B
